seoTitle: Mount Sheridan vs Mount St John — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Sheridan and Mount St John. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Sheridan vs Mount St John: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Sheridan and Mount St John.
heading: Mount Sheridan vs Mount St John — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Sheridan, Queensland and Mount St John, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Sheridan has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Mount St John (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Sheridan or Mount St John safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Sheridan has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Mount St John scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Sheridan scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Mount St John scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Sheridan or Mount St John?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Sheridan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
